items.title: Likelihood functions
items.description: L(θ) = P(data | θ) is the objective MLE maximizes; this topic applies argmax to the likelihood (or log-likelihood) built from a chosen parametric model.

items.title: Gradient descent
items.description: When no closed-form MLE exists, minimize negative log-likelihood −ℓ(θ) with θ ← θ − η∇(−ℓ(θ))—gradient ascent on ℓ(θ) using the same optimization machinery.

items.title: Linear regression
items.description: Ordinary least squares on Gaussian noise targets is MLE for the conditional mean μ(x) = wᵀx + b when homoscedastic variance is treated as a nuisance parameter.

items.title: Cross-entropy
items.description: Average cross-entropy over labels equals negative log-likelihood under a Categorical (softmax) model; minimizing cross-entropy performs MLE of class probabilities.
